hii can anyone that played Sangas deepdive explain what happened at the end? i dont get it 😭
Keeper was "replaying" the life of Sangas servant, so its implied sanga killed her
i see, i thought she implied that sangas servant was the real camille
they swap the names around for creative reasons but Sanga is based on the real Camille
wait im confused, so sanga is the real camille then she killed her servant? then what does the statue do
im a bit confused on the story overall
its vague on purpose because sangas artistic tastes are like that, but there's a few ways you could interpret her ending

1. the statue is sanga and she transformed into it because her alternative fate is being dissoluted 
2. the statue is a really convincing replica and sanga killed (or disappeared) herself for her aequor art tastes
3. she already awakened at some point during the events so it might be explained by her abilities 

pick your favorite option
but clearly the statue serves both as her testimony towards her affection for aequor and to spite Auguste
ah i see i see, 1 is my headcannon from the start but 2 and 3 seem possible. thank you!!
can u also help me with gramona story, like what is she trying to fix?
english is not my first language so i dont really get the nuances in the story

Sanga is based on the real Camille
but Camille in Morimens is the name of Sanga’s maid
so basically at the end of her deepdive it’s a metaphor for her killing her human self and embrace the madness
gramona comes from an alternate timeline where keeper dies in the prologue, so what she means by "fixing" is repeating the same day until she can save the keeper
ohhh
ohhhhh
i see i see
thanks everyone

8 years and counting
I increasingly feel like
Ramona = Mash, William = Dr. Roman
For the Keeper

Ramona is definitely made to be the heroine type that most gachas have though I like her infinitely more than Mash

William has the ||important authority figure who died trying to save the day|| going for him but I wouldn't really equate him to Romani since their role and general narrative quirks (Romani spent most of part 1 as the butt monkey comic relief who seemed kind of bad at his new job even though he was secretly doing a lot behind the scenes while William is always treated super seriously as a gifted investigator)
Liz twice on standard
True
Who should i pull sorel, e2 tawil or murphy
Tawil and it's not even close
I mean like the important older guy who the protag looks up to who then ||dies|| and the protag is now chasing after them trying to bring them back while people wait for them to become playable
Yeah
I guess I can see it from that angle though id honestly say Dexter has more in common with Romani than him
Down to being the heroine character's adoptive dad
The ||reveal that he's N|| aside
Murphy and Sorel can drop from standard banner (though at a lower chance) and tawil is still true limited for the time being. Ans tawil is stronger
Lol
Dexter is hot i wish he wasn’t a ded npc
Unlikely we’re getting dexter playable because when N becomes playable one day he probably won’t look like Dexter
Or N can shapeshift every time he uses his ult /j
I like how keeper’s feelings towards william evolves it makes them feel more like an individual character
Granted they never really wrote Keeper as much of a standard self insert even early on
True
They actually have solid preferences and opinions on the people and the world around them outside of generic "wants to do the right thing" or "meme response"
The story has narration in second person like “you hear something” “you turn around to see” but keeper is also fleshed out. I prefer this
Like if player wants to self insert they can but it doesn’t mean mc has to be a boring blank slate

That's part of why I liked their relationship with Ramona a lot even though I disliked Mash and a lot of FGO's heroine of the chapter writing, because it actually feels like they're two people who have an established history and are used to bouncing jokes off one another
A lot of FGO’s master love waifus have mid writing ngl
They may even be popular but the writing is mid
The greatest piece of keeper characterization is chapter 9 || final will ||
And they dubbed that goodbye letter
FGO is kinda strange in that the characters themselves are pretty fine even good but when their writing connects to their relationship with Guda I'm infinitely less enthusiastic
One I only put highly is Castoria but she also had an entire visual novel for herself in lb6
It’s really moving if you think about it because devs really thought they were gonna EOS when they wrote that ending
It's even worse for characters who don't even interact with Guda in the story first to justify it because they go from interesting characters to post summoning watch bait for no reason

Yeah the ||final will|| made me cry, and laugh because what the hell do you mean you're still bitching about how much you hate uni studies ||on something you know is gonna be read at your funeral!!!||

Honestly in morimens some characters have that “0 interaction in main story/psyche deepdive but suddenly head over heels for keeper in their frenzy/voicelines” too. Like… castor and pollux. Keeper doesn’t even show up in their psyche deepdive stories? But they call keeper my benefactor my light etc
I can only guess keeper gets involved later but they havent wrote about it yet lol
Keeper be like: If their reading this, means they can't awaken my silver and I can write what I want without repercussions

Castoria is interesting because compared to most other heroine types in the game her dynamic can be easily read as platonic (in LB6 at least, less so come summer)  so it allows their bonding to feel more organic
I chalk that one up to a consequence of EOS scare booting Castor out of his main story relevance
Morimen deeply suffers of not enough story time for some things
We could've stretched arc for atleast 12 chapters
But that's an eos issue
I wish they had more 3.5 chapter
Since people will skip over special ops
Yeah tbh compared to arc1 id say they still havnt fully recovered from eos, they’re still short on staff compared w before so arc 2 main story is released slower
Also the big side stories special ops has come to a halt, psyche deepdives are smaller in terms of scale

Arc 2 so far also seems to prefer keeping chapter conflicts relatively one and done (though there's an overarching plot to work towards) while barring Thais chapter (because EOS) arc 1 stories tended to be like 2 or 3 chapters mini arcs?
Yeah
Arc 2 is like one major character tied to each chapter
I guess with how slow production is their wary of letting narratives be stuck in scheduling limbo
Arc 2 gdoll thing. Huh, things are really different on a fundamental scale
Though on this when I rolled for Pollux and saw the my light thing, I actually assumed the deep dive would end with him and Castor bumping into them and being invited to Mythag
Either that or them being assigned to watch over the two, kind of like how Mizuki is handled in Arknights
Im glad main story writing quality is still there
Yeah a thing I find neat about Arc 2 is the random ways characters seem to switch. And yet, somehow, ||Juliette is still a horrible person lol||
There had been a couple event stories I considered mid (1.5 anni) but the main story and psyche deepdives are fine
also we getting translation now
